name: Thai Curry Penne
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon Thai Curry Base (see below), heaping
fresh basil - cut into thin strips
recipeInstructions:
Thai Curry Base
1 tablespoon unsalted butter
1/3 cup onion - chopped
1/2 teaspoon garlic - chopped
3 teaspoons mild curry powder
1/3 cup Marsala wine
1 Granny Smith apple - peel, core, chop
1 cup chicken stock
salt and freshly ground pepper
1/4 cup fish sauce (Nuoc Mam)
4 1/2 teaspoons Thai red curry paste
Tomato Chutney
1/2 cup rice wine vinegar
2 teaspoons ginger - grated
1/4 cup brown sugar - packed
juice of 1/2 lemon
1 can pear tomatoes in juice - drain and chop
2 whole cloves
1 cinnamon stick
salt and freshly ground pepper
In a saucepan combine the cream, coconut milk, and Thai curry base; reduce over high heat until thickened and about one-half of original volume. Add the crab meat and cooked penne and toss well. Place the pasta on a plate, and top with tomato chutney, broiled scallops and basil.
To make the Thai Curry Base:
1. In a saute pan melt the butter, add the onion, garlic and curry powder and saute, stirring occasionally, until the onions are soft.
2. Add the Marsala, stir well and reduce by one-half.
3. Add the apples and stock, salt and pepper; bring to a boil and simmer for 45 minutes.
4. Puree and cool. Stir in the fish sauce and Thai red curry paste, mix well and set aside. (Makes about one cup, or 10 tablespoons.)
To make the Tomato Chutney:
1. In a saucepan combine the rice vinegar, ginger, brown sugar and lemon juice; simmer for 5 minutes.
2. Add the tomatoes, cloves and cinnamon stick; season with salt and pepper. Simmer for 30 minutes, cool, remove cloves and cinnamon stick and set aside.
